name: Reflexive Verbs
summary: Reflexive verbs describe actions you do to yourself, using reflexive pronouns.
explanation:
Reflexive verbs describe an action the subject does to itself: chamar-se (to be named), levantar-se (to get up), sentir-se (to feel). In the dictionary they end in -se.

The reflexive pronouns are me (myself), se (yourself/himself/herself), nos (ourselves), se (themselves). In Brazilian Portuguese the pronoun normally comes before the verb: eu me chamo, ele se levanta.

Many feeling and daily-routine verbs are reflexive. In casual Brazilian speech the pronoun is sometimes dropped, but learning the correct form first is best.

examples.en: We had a lot of fun.
They get ready to go out.

commonMistakes: Forgetting the reflexive pronoun. Chamo Lucas means 'I call Lucas', not 'My name is Lucas'.
Using the European placement (chamo-me) when Brazilian usage is eu me chamo.
Confusing se for 'yourself' with se as part of 'if'.
Dropping the pronoun in writing where it should appear.
Mixing the pronoun with the wrong subject; eu takes me, not se.
tip: In Brazil the reflexive pronoun usually sits before the verb, so eu me chamo sounds more natural than chamo-me.
